access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (PBS-covered drugs via EPS-UPC) + reimbursement (non-PBS via MIPRES/presupuestos máximos) + judicial (tutela for excluded or delayed drugs)
procurement_channels: PBS (Plan de Beneficios en Salud) — drugs financed through UPC capitation paid to EPS by ADRES. Non-PBS — financed through presupuestos máximos via MIPRES prescription. Excluded technologies require tutela or out-of-pocket. ~5% of total health budget routed through non-PBS/MIPRES channel.
key_actors: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social (MINSALUD) — issues Resolution 2718/2024 defining PBS for 2025. IETS (Instituto de Evaluación Tecnológica en Salud) — health technology assessment. ADRES — disburses UPC and presupuestos máximos. EPS — operationalize formulary at point of dispensing. Comisión de Beneficios y Tarifas — historical role in formulary updates.
formulary_coverage: Resolution 2718/2024 (effective Jan 1, 2025) maintains PBS coverage for high-cost diseases including cancer, chronic kidney disease, orphan diseases, autoimmune. Conventional cytotoxic oncology drugs (cisplatin, doxorubicin, carboplatin) are PBS-covered. Trastuzumab IV covered for approved HER2+ indications (early/metastatic breast cancer; advanced gastric cancer per label). Annex 1 of Resolution 2718/2024 lists funded medications. PBS does NOT cover: medications without INVIMA approval, experimental treatments, treatments without scientific support. [uncertain on exact list of newer targeted/immunotherapy oncology drugs included vs excluded for 2025]
judicial_access_volume: Health tutelas ~265,000 (2024) → ~312,500 (2025); ~34% health-related; ~24% of Defensoría-accompanied health tutelas relate to medication denial. Tutela is the dominant access mechanism for high-cost oncology drugs not on PBS or where EPS rejects MIPRES prescription. ~100 specific treatments/services moved to exclusion list under reform of 2026, expanding tutela demand.
registration_timeline_generic: Not applicable (INVIMA function, not PBS scope).
registration_authority: INVIMA registers; MINSALUD decides PBS inclusion through annual UPC/PBS resolutions.
biosimilar_pathway: Not directly part of PBS; once an INVIMA-approved biosimilar exists, EPS may substitute under PBS coverage. IETS recommendations and price negotiations drive uptake.
reform_status: enacted (Resolution 2718/2024 in force Jan 1, 2025). Subsequent broader health reform partially blocked: Decree 0858/2025 suspended by Council of State (Oct 23, 2025); congressional reform blocked Dec 2025. PBS reform of 2026 reportedly excluded ~100 treatments, increasing reliance on tutela.
currency_mechanism: Floating COP — affects pricing of imported oncology drugs included in PBS.
pharma_import_cost_impact: [uncertain] — Colombia spent COP ~28.9 trillion on medicines in 2024 (ACEMI estimate) without proportional access improvement; FX-linked cost growth a contributing factor.
financial_mechanism: PBS-covered drugs paid via UPC capitation from ADRES to EPS. Non-PBS drugs paid via presupuestos máximos through MIPRES system. Co-payments and moderating quotas (cuotas moderadoras) apply per income tier.
payer_debt_crisis: Indirect — formulary expansion outpaces UPC adjustment, contributing to EPS-IPS-pharma debt cascade (>COP 24-32.9 trillion total; COP 4.2 trillion pharma).
shortage_rate: [uncertain on PBS-specific shortage rate]; INVIMA reports 8 medicines in shortage state, 5 at risk, 181 monitored (Dec 2024).
shortage_causes: PBS coverage exists on paper but de-facto shortages emerge from EPS financial fragility, distributor supply restrictions, INVIMA backlog. Tutela volume signals access gap exceeding formal shortage list.
shortage_monitoring_system: MINSALUD desabastecimiento page references INVIMA tracker; new SISCAATS in development.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: [uncertain] — no PBS-specific cisplatin/doxorubicin/carboplatin/trastuzumab shortage event publicly listed in 2023-2025; tutela claims and PQRS (52,655 in Jan 2025 peak) suggest patient-level access failures.
source_citation: 1) MINSALUD, Resolución 2718 de 2024, https://www.minsalud.gov.co/Normatividad_Nuevo/Resoluci%C3%B3n%20No%202718%20de%202024.pdf. 2) Neuroeconomix, 'Actualización del PBS en Colombia para el 2025: Resolución 2718 de 2024,' 2024. 3) ConsultorSalud, 'Actualización de Servicios y Tecnologías de Salud Financiados con la UPC para 2025,' 2024. 4) Law 2360 of 2024 (cancer protection statute). 5) Sentencia T-377 de 2024 (Constitutional Court).
source_type_granular: government_primary (Resolution 2718/2024) + legal_regulatory_tracker (Neuroeconomix, ConsultorSalud) + judicial (Constitutional Court rulings)
data_reliability: high for the resolution itself; medium for translation into specific oncology drug-level coverage details
